judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 covers all four expectations (including the staleness/re-check caveat on the snapshot timestamp), while Report 2 misses expectation (d) entirely — Report 1 is more complete overall.

report_1.evidence: One scoring call per transcript × 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = **≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)** for a full run ... | northpeak / nimbus-large | primary (**default**) | **640,000** | needs 9.36M | **Short by ~8.72M — run is ~14.6× the remaining allowance; would blow the window**
